findings.message: T001 undercounts the `check_type_signals` ripple. `libs/domain/src/tddd/consistency.rs` already has 8 dedicated `test_check_type_signals_*` cases plus `test_consistency_partitions_secondary_adapter_as_type`, so the documented “7 tests” misses real call sites and will leave at least the undeclared-yellow and secondary-adapter cases uncovered. The same undercount is repeated in `spec.json:76`.

T001 only enumerates the three production call sites and compresses the `consistency.rs` test ripple into `8 tests`; it does not list the seven existing `check_type_signals` test call sites required by the review brief, so the signature-change ripple is not fully specified.

T001's verification checklist says there are 7 existing `check_type_signals` tests, but `metadata.json`/`spec.json` enumerate 8 existing tests and the current `libs/domain/src/tddd/consistency.rs` test block has 8 `test_check_type_signals_*` cases. The checklist undercounts the ripple and leaves one existing test case untracked.

T003's acceptance text says there are 6 unit tests, but it only names five conditions; the sixth case from `metadata.json` (`single-cluster cross_edges empty`) is missing, and the same omission is echoed in `verification.md`. That leaves the ClusterPlan acceptance scope under-specified.

T006 still requires a `deduplicate_typestate_edges` default decision even though the final 6-task scope never includes that option in `spec.json`'s in-scope items or acceptance criteria. This leaves the SSoT task list with an orphan verification target copied from the older 8-task planner output, so implementers cannot tell whether that knob is actually part of Phase 2(K) or already deferred.

The T004 safety requirement is sourced to `.claude/rules/10-guardrails.md §Symlink safety`, but that section does not exist in the referenced file. Because this acceptance criterion relies on that citation as its convention source, the spec currently contains a broken reference for one of the symlink-deletion guard requirements.

The archived planner notes T001 as a "3 crates + 7 test sites" change, but `libs/domain/src/tddd/consistency.rs` currently has 8 existing `test_check_type_signals_*` tests to update. That factual miscount understates the signature-ripple work in the research artifact by one existing test.

The Q3 rationale still cites `.claude/rules/10-guardrails.md` §symlink safety, but that section does not exist in the referenced file. This leaves the archived planner doc with a broken convention reference even though the final plan/spec already moved the symlink-safety source to `knowledge/conventions/security.md §Symlink Rejection in Infrastructure Adapters`.

The planner archive contradicts its own task table on execution order: line 22 says "bug fix first, then edges, then clustering," but the summary on line 14 and the ordered tasks table place clustering work in T003-T004 before edges in T005. This leaves the planning artifact internally inconsistent about dependencies and rollout order.

`spec.json` still describes `T006` as adding a new ADR deferral note (`"ADR 2026-04-16-2200 に ... note を追加する"`), but `metadata.json` and `verification.md` were updated to say `§Phase 2 Scope Update §S1-§S5` already exists from planning and that T006 now only supplements `§S4/§S5` with empirical data. That leaves the authoritative spec out of sync with the task description/checklist for the same work item.

T006 is still described here as adding the ADR note from scratch (`ADR 2026-04-16-2200 に ... note を追加する`), but `metadata.json` now says `§Phase 2 Scope Update §S1-§S5` was already added during planning and `verification.md` marks that existence check as done. That leaves the planning artifacts disagreeing about whether T006 still creates the addendum or only supplements `§S4/§S5` with empirical data.

The T006 checklist requires a `cluster_depth 1 vs 2` readability comparison, but the documented manual verification flow only runs `track type-graph` with `--cluster-depth 2`. As written, the verification procedure cannot produce the depth-1 output needed to satisfy the stated check.

T006 is only partially realigned. `spec.json` still defines the task as adding a new ADR note for DRIFT-01 / entry-point / auto-render deferrals, but the updated `metadata.json`, `plan.md`, and `verification.md` now treat `§Phase 2 Scope Update §S1-§S5` as already present and say the remaining work is only empirical reinforcement in `§S4/§S5`. Because `spec.md` is rendered from this stale source-of-truth entry, the track still tells implementers to do work that the verification checklist already marks as done, reintroducing the task/acceptance mismatch this round was meant to remove.

§S1 upgrades an unsupported policy claim into the ADR: it says `.claude/rules/04-coding-principles.md` allows the codebase to prefer `enum + fn transition(self, ...) -> Result<Self, E>` for small state machines, but that convention explicitly says any state transition should prefer typestate, and a repo-wide search in `libs/`/`apps/` does not show such transition APIs in the current codebase. Because this addendum is now cited as the Blue source for the scope reduction, the ADR is recording a rationale that the cited convention and the codebase do not substantiate.

`verification.md` still describes T006 as adding an “ADR note” and says the postponement rationale/re-evaluation conditions will be reflected by T006, but the updated metadata/spec now treat `§Phase 2 Scope Update §S1-§S5` as already added during planning and limit T006 to supplementing `§S4/§S5` with measurements. That leaves the verification artifact internally inconsistent about what work remains for T006.

The new addendum shrinks Phase 2 to scope (K) and defers Entry/DRIFT-01/orphan/auto-render, but the unchanged `Phased Scope Summary` still says Phase 2 is `cluster+drift`, has `Entry` = `あり`, and adds `--check-drift`. That leaves the ADR with two conflicting definitions of Phase 2 scope, and future readers can easily pick the stale summary table instead of the new addendum.

Round 2 makes `deduplicate_typestate_edges` a documented T006 deliverable, but the track still never requires the option itself to be implemented. `§S4` says T004 will add `TypeGraphRenderOptions::deduplicate_typestate_edges: bool`, yet T004's task list remains unchanged and the acceptance here only asks for a recorded 'default judgment'. That means T006 can pass by documenting a default for a flag the track never had to ship.

T006's acceptance criteria no longer cover the task's stated remaining work. `metadata.json` and `verification.md` now say T006 must add empirical data to ADR `§S4/§S5`, but acceptance criterion 9 only checks that `§S2` already records the scope-K deferrals. Because that ADR content is already present from planning, T006 can satisfy the spec without doing the new `§S4/§S5` reinforcement at all.

The new depth-comparison verification flow overwrites the first render before it can be compared. Step 2 generates `--cluster-depth 2`, then step 2b reruns the command against the same track output with `--cluster-depth 1 --force`, so the depth-2 artifacts are gone by the time step 3 asks for a readability comparison. As written, reviewers cannot perform the required `depth 1 vs 2` side-by-side check.

`test_read_type_catalogue_found` only exercises the legacy default filename (`domain-types.json`). The behavior added in this round is returning the resolved filename from `architecture-rules.json`; if `read_type_catalogue` regressed to always returning `<layer_id>-types.json` after successfully reading an override file, this test would still pass. Add a case with a non-default `tddd.catalogue_file` (and ideally a rules-file parse/fetch failure case) so the new adapter contract is actually covered.

The updated `test_read_type_catalogue_found` still exercises only the default `domain-types.json` path. This round’s behavioral change is specifically about honoring `tddd.catalogue_file` overrides and returning the resolved filename in `Found((doc, filename))`, so an implementation that always returned `{layer_id}-types.json` would still pass. Add a fixture with `architecture-rules.json` pointing at a non-default catalogue filename and assert that `read_type_catalogue()` returns that override.

All `verify_from_spec_json` fixtures still hard-code `catalogue_file: "domain-types.json"`. Because `evaluate_layer_catalogue()` now forwards `binding.catalogue_file()` into `check_type_signals`, the CI path has no regression test for a non-default override; a bug that fell back to the default filename in diagnostics would still pass here. Add a Stage 2 fixture that uses a non-default catalogue file (for example `usecase-types.json`) and assert the finding mentions that configured filename.

`catalogue_file` is rebuilt here as the default `<layer_id>-types.json`, but `TrackBlobReader::read_type_catalogue` can resolve an explicit `tddd.catalogue_file` override from `architecture-rules.json`. For a layer configured with a non-default file name, the merge gate will read the correct file yet report any Red/Yellow findings against a different, nonexistent filename, unlike the CI path which passes the resolved `binding.catalogue_file()`.

P1: `catalogue_file` is rebuilt as `format!("{layer_id}-types.json")`, but `read_type_catalogue()` can read an explicit `tddd.catalogue_file` override from `architecture-rules.json`. When a layer uses a non-default catalogue filename, Stage 2 findings will point at the wrong file name, which breaks the new contract that `check_type_signals` diagnostics name the actual catalogue being validated.
